pi_items.source: Downloaded from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/__moin_import__/attachments/SampleCaptures/sip-rtp-g711.pcap; verified as valid pcap file (195K); confirmed on Wireshark official SampleCaptures collection
Task setup script creates directory /home/ga/Documents/captures; verifier.py expects file at this path; export_result.sh validates existence at this location

Analyzed PCAP file structure: 840 RTP packets × 172 bytes payload per packet = 144,480 bytes RTP data + 28-byte Sun audio header = ~144.5 KB. Verified using tshark packet analysis. Wireshark's RTP Player will export these packets as Sun audio format. Actual file size far exceeds the 50KB minimum.

Verified through Wireshark documentation search; confirmed that Wireshark's RTP audio extraction saves to Sun Audio (.au) format for G.711 PCMU codec. The file command identifies this as 'Sun/NeXT audio file'. RTP Player saves with proper Sun audio header (magic number 0x2E736E64).

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the real sip-rtp-g711.pcap file from Wireshark's official sample captures collection (195KB). The capture contains a complete SIP call with 840 RTP packets encoded in G.711 PCMU audio codec, totaling approximately 144.5KB of payload data. When extracted via Wireshark's RTP Player and saved as Sun Audio format (.au), the output file will be approximately 141-145 KB, well exceeding the stated minimum of 50KB. The expected file format is Sun/NeXT audio, which is the standard export format from Wireshark for G.711 audio streams.
